name: Black-Scholes European call option price
task: Create /tmp/eval_bs/bs.py with a function bs_call(S, K, T, r, sigma) returning the Black-Scholes price of a European CALL option. S=spot, K=strike, T=time to maturity in years, r=continuously-compounded risk-free rate (decimal), sigma=annualized volatility (decimal). Use the standard formula: C = S*N(d1) - K*exp(-r*T)*N(d2), with d1 = (ln(S/K) + (r + sigma^2/2)*T) / (sigma*sqrt(T)) and d2 = d1 - sigma*sqrt(T). The cumulative normal N can be computed as 0.5 * (1 + erf(x / sqrt(2))) — math.erf is available in stdlib. Pure Python, no scipy.

validation.script: import importlib.util; spec = importlib.util.spec_from_file_location('m', '/tmp/eval_bs/bs.py'); mod = importlib.util.module_from_spec(spec); spec.loader.exec_module(mod); c = mod.bs_call; ref = c(100, 100, 1.0, 0.05, 0.2); assert abs(ref - 10.4505835721856) < 1e-4, f'ATM 1y got {ref}'; assert abs(c(100, 110, 0.5, 0.03, 0.25) - 4.336) < 1e-2, f'OTM 6m got {c(100,110,0.5,0.03,0.25)}'; assert abs(c(50, 100, 1.0, 0.05, 0.2) - 0.0) < 0.01, f'deep OTM got {c(50,100,1.0,0.05,0.2)}'; assert abs(c(150, 100, 1.0, 0.05, 0.2) - (150 - 100*2.718281828459045**(-0.05))) < 1.0, 'deep ITM ~ S - K*exp(-rT)'; print('OK')
